note: run_eval.py trigger detection fails for all positive cases (0/15) due to skill registration gap in the eval harness — the temporary command file approach doesn't register plugin skills correctly. All 18 negative cases pass (zero false positives). Manual description variant evaluation performed instead.

harness_results.note: All failures are false negatives from harness limitation, not description quality

variants.description: Project-level configuration via .ai/project.yml — profile presets (work/personal), hook flag overrides, dev commands, and git conventions. Explains the unified config system: how to bind a profile, which flags are overridable vs safety-locked, and where to find the full schema. Use when setting up a new project, switching profiles, configuring hook behavior, checking which profile is active, understanding override precedence, or asking about project.yml fields.
variants.strengths: Comprehensive coverage, mentions both profiles, lists all config areas, clear use-when clause
variants.weaknesses: Long, explanation-heavy, 'unified config system' is jargon

variants.description: Configure project behavior via .ai/project.yml — choose work or personal profile, override hook flags (TDD gate, tracing, verification, doc checkpoint), set dev commands and git conventions. Safety flags (scope_guard, push_protection) cannot be disabled. Use when setting up project config, switching profiles, disabling specific hooks, checking active profile settings, or understanding which flags are overridable.
variants.strengths: Names all 4 overridable flags explicitly, mentions safety constraint, action-oriented verbs
variants.weaknesses: Still long, lists flags which may date if flags change

variants.description: Project configuration via .ai/project.yml. Profile presets (work/personal) control which hooks run — TDD gate, tracing, doc checkpoint, verification are overridable per project; scope_guard and push_protection are locked. Use when configuring project behavior, choosing a profile, overriding hook flags, or checking active settings.
variants.weaknesses: Doesn't mention dev commands, git conventions, or the full schema pointer

recommendation: v1_original retained — it has the broadest trigger surface and the eval confirmed zero false positives. v3_concise is the strongest alternative if context budget becomes a concern. The eval harness issue should be fixed separately (logged to opportunities.md).
